Defines macros for format specifiers of integer types for printf and scanf functions.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines a set of macros for format specifiers used in `printf`-style functions. These macros are designed to provide a consistent way to format integer types of various sizes, such as 8-bit, 16-bit, 32-bit, and 64-bit integers, as well as pointer-sized integers. The file includes conditional logic to determine the appropriate format specifier based on the size of the integer type, which can vary depending on the compiler and platform. The macros are defined for different integer types and their corresponding format specifiers, such as decimal (`d`), integer (`i`), octal (`o`), unsigned (`u`), and hexadecimal (`x` and `X`).

The header file uses preprocessor directives to check if certain macros are already defined, and if not, it defines them using the appropriate format specifier macros. This ensures that the format specifiers are only defined once, preventing redefinition errors. The file also includes comments that provide guidance on how to verify the types used by the compiler, specifically for the ARM GCC toolchain. The header file is intended to be included in other C source files to provide a standardized way to handle integer formatting across different platforms and compilers.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`
